Ishigami.name: Ishigami
Ishigami.primary_use_case: SA method validation; interaction effect testing
Ishigami.known_challenges: X3 has zero first-order but nonzero total-order index
Ishigami.reference_title: An importance quantification technique in uncertainty analysis for computer models
Ishigami.reference_journal: Proc. 1st Int. Symp. Uncertainty Modelling and Analysis
Ishigami.reference_full: Ishigami, T., & Homma, T. (1990). An importance quantification technique in uncertainty analysis for computer models. In Proc. 1st Int. Symp. Uncertainty Modelling and Analysis (pp. 398-403). IEEE.
Ishigami.notes: De facto standard SA benchmark. The non-zero b*X3^4*sin(X1) term creates a pure interaction with no first-order effect for X3. Extremely widely used.

SobolG.name: SobolG
SobolG.sensitivity_structure: product form; analytical decomposition via Hoeffding
SobolG.primary_use_case: Benchmarking with tunable importance; analytical validation
SobolG.known_challenges: Non-smooth kink at X_i=0.5; affects PCE convergence
SobolG.reference_title: About the use of rank transformation in sensitivity analysis of model output
SobolG.reference_full: Saltelli, A., & Sobol, I. M. (1995). About the use of rank transformation in sensitivity analysis of model output. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 50(3), 225-239.
SobolG.notes: G-function family with tunable a_i. With a=[0,1,4.5,9,99,99,99,99] the first four inputs are dominant. Exact Sobol indices available via Hoeffding product decomposition.

Borehole.name: Borehole
Borehole.primary_use_case: Groundwater flow modeling; sensitivity analysis validation
Borehole.known_challenges: Input distributions are non-uniform (log-normal for r); bounds approximation common
Borehole.reference_title: Sensitivity/uncertainty analysis of a borehole scenario comparing Latin Hypercube Sampling and deterministic sensitivity approaches
Borehole.reference_full: Harper, W. V., & Gupta, S. K. (1983). Sensitivity/uncertainty analysis of a borehole scenario comparing Latin Hypercube Sampling and deterministic sensitivity approaches. BMI/ONWI-516, Battelle Memorial Institute.
Borehole.notes: Classic hydrogeology benchmark. Widely used in emulation/surrogate modeling literature. The logarithmic and rational structure creates moderate nonlinearity.

Piston.name: Piston
Piston.formula_latex: C(t) = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{M}{k + S^2 P_0 V_0 T_a/(T_0 V^2)}}
Piston.known_challenges: Physical constraint: volume V must be positive
Piston.reference_title: Modeling data from computer experiments: an empirical comparison of kriging with MARS and projection pursuit regression
Piston.reference_full: Ben-Ari, E. N., & Steinberg, D. M. (2007). Modeling data from computer experiments: an empirical comparison of kriging with MARS and projection pursuit regression. Quality Engineering, 19(4), 327-338.
Piston.notes: Piston cycle time model. The square-root structure creates bounded output. Commonly used for emulator comparison studies.

WingWeight.name: WingWeight
WingWeight.known_challenges: 10-dimensional; power-law interactions make PCE convergence slow
WingWeight.reference_full: Forrester, A., Sobester, A., & Keane, A. (2008). Engineering Design via Surrogate Modelling. Wiley.
WingWeight.notes: Aircraft wing weight formula from aeronautical engineering. Power-law product structure (partially log-linear). Widely used in metamodeling benchmarks.

OTLCircuit.name: OTLCircuit
OTLCircuit.known_challenges: Rational structure; output depends on complex interaction of all 6 inputs
OTLCircuit.reference_title: Modeling data from computer experiments: an empirical comparison of kriging with MARS and projection pursuit regression
OTLCircuit.reference_full: Ben-Ari, E. N., & Steinberg, D. M. (2007). Modeling data from computer experiments. Quality Engineering, 19(4), 327-338.
OTLCircuit.notes: OTL (output transformer less) push-pull circuit. Output is midpoint voltage. Classic small-dimensionality electronic benchmark.

Morris.name: Morris
Morris.sensitivity_structure: designed interaction structure; Morris screening target
Morris.primary_use_case: Morris screening method validation; high-d SA
Morris.known_challenges: 20 inputs with constructed 4th-order interactions; curvature from w=2(x-0.5) transformation
Morris.reference_title: Factorial sampling plans for preliminary computational experiments
Morris.reference_full: Morris, M. D. (1991). Factorial sampling plans for preliminary computational experiments. Technometrics, 33(2), 161-174.
Morris.notes: Canonical benchmark for elementary effects (Morris screening). Nonlinearity arises from omega_i = 2(X_i - 0.5) substitution for some inputs.

LinearModel.name: LinearModel
LinearModel.primary_use_case: Baseline/sanity check; affine transform commutativity test
LinearModel.reference_title: Making best use of model evaluations to compute sensitivity indices
LinearModel.reference_full: Saltelli, A. (2002). Making best use of model evaluations to compute sensitivity indices. Computer Physics Communications, 145(2), 280-297.
LinearModel.notes: Pure linear model. S1=ST by construction (no interactions). Affine transforms commute exactly. Used as ground truth for estimator validation.

AdditiveQuadratic.name: AdditiveQuadratic
AdditiveQuadratic.sensitivity_structure: purely additive; no interactions; quadratic curvature
AdditiveQuadratic.primary_use_case: Closed-form SA validation; nonlinear but non-interactive benchmark
AdditiveQuadratic.reference_full: Saltelli, A., Ratto, M., Andres, T., et al. (2008). Global Sensitivity Analysis: The Primer. Wiley.
AdditiveQuadratic.notes: Purely additive quadratic. S1=ST exact. Tests sensitivity of SA estimators to mild nonlinearity without interaction effects.

PCETestFunction.name: PCETestFunction
PCETestFunction.sensitivity_structure: Legendre PCE; known interaction structure (X1,X2) and (X1,X3)
PCETestFunction.primary_use_case: PCE method validation; known Sobol structure
PCETestFunction.reference_title: Global sensitivity analysis using polynomial chaos expansions
PCETestFunction.reference_full: Sudret, B. (2008). Global sensitivity analysis using polynomial chaos expansions. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 93(7), 964-979.
PCETestFunction.notes: Designed for exact PCE/Sobol validation. Legendre orthogonality gives exact variance decomposition from PCE coefficients.

Friedman.name: Friedman
Friedman.primary_use_case: Variable selection; dimension reduction; feature importance
Friedman.known_challenges: X1 and X2 are entangled via sin(pi*X1*X2) interaction
Friedman.reference_full: Friedman, J. H. (1991). Multivariate adaptive regression splines. Annals of Statistics, 19(1), 1-67.
Friedman.notes: Originally proposed for MARS regression. X6-X10 are pure noise. Widely used to test variable selection and screening methods.

OakleyOHagan.name: OakleyOHagan
OakleyOHagan.formula_latex: f(X) = a_1^T x + a_2^T \sin(x) + a_3^T \cos(x) + x^T M x
OakleyOHagan.sensitivity_structure: linear + sinusoidal + cosine + quadratic interaction matrix M
OakleyOHagan.primary_use_case: GP emulation; Bayesian SA; high-dimensional benchmark
OakleyOHagan.known_challenges: 15 inputs; 9 inputs have negligible effect; normal input distributions
OakleyOHagan.reference_title: Probabilistic sensitivity analysis of complex models: a Bayesian approach
OakleyOHagan.reference_journal: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, Series B
OakleyOHagan.reference_full: Oakley, J. E., & O'Hagan, A. (2004). Probabilistic sensitivity analysis of complex models: a Bayesian approach. Journal of the Royal Statistical Society: Series B, 66(3), 751-769.
OakleyOHagan.notes: Purpose-designed for Bayesian GP emulation SA. Standard benchmarks from Oakley & O'Hagan (2004) using their published a1, a2, a3, M matrices.

MoonHerrera.name: MoonHerrera
MoonHerrera.sensitivity_structure: active subspace; exponential coupling; all inputs interact
MoonHerrera.primary_use_case: Active subspace method testing; dimension reduction
MoonHerrera.known_challenges: All 20 inputs interact via the exponential; high interaction order
MoonHerrera.reference_title: Designing computer experiments for simulation-based methods
MoonHerrera.reference_full: Moon, H., Dean, A. M., & Santner, T. J. (2012). Two-stage sensitivity-based group screening in computer experiments. Technometrics, 54(4), 376-387.
MoonHerrera.notes: Exponential function of a linear combination. Active subspace is 1D (direction w). Exact Sobol indices via moment-generating function of Uniform(0,1).

CornerPeak.name: CornerPeak
CornerPeak.analytical_S1_formula: S1_i via 1D Gauss-Legendre quadrature on product-form decomposition
CornerPeak.sensitivity_structure: rational power; all-input interaction via denominator
CornerPeak.primary_use_case: Numerical quadrature integration benchmarking; SA comparison
CornerPeak.known_challenges: Peaked behavior near origin; c_i controls difficulty
CornerPeak.reference_journal: Tools, Methods, and Languages for Scientific and Engineering Computation
CornerPeak.reference_full: Genz, A. (1984). Testing multidimensional integration routines. In Tools, Methods, and Languages for Scientific and Engineering Computation (pp. 81-94). North-Holland.
CornerPeak.notes: One of the Genz test functions for multidimensional integration. The corner peak function is strongly peaked near X=0. All inputs interact via the denominator power.

ProductPeak.name: ProductPeak
ProductPeak.analytical_S1_formula: S1_i via Gauss-Legendre: E[g_i^2(X_i)] * prod_{j!=i}(E[g_j(X_j)])^2 / V_total
ProductPeak.primary_use_case: Numerical integration benchmarking; SA for product-form functions
ProductPeak.known_challenges: Sharp peaks for small c_i values; high variance
ProductPeak.reference_journal: Tools, Methods, and Languages for Scientific and Engineering Computation
ProductPeak.reference_full: Genz, A. (1984). Testing multidimensional integration routines. In Tools, Methods, and Languages for Scientific and Engineering Computation (pp. 81-94). North-Holland.
ProductPeak.notes: Genz product peak function. Lorentzian (Cauchy-like) factors per dimension. Exact S1 via Gauss-Legendre quadrature on each 1D marginal.

Rosenbrock.name: Rosenbrock
Rosenbrock.sensitivity_structure: chained pairwise interactions; banana valley; near-zero Hessian at minimum
Rosenbrock.primary_use_case: Optimization algorithm testing; SA for highly nonlinear functions
Rosenbrock.known_challenges: Extremely large variance; heavy tail; Saltelli N must be very large
Rosenbrock.reference_title: An automatic method for finding the greatest or least value of a function
Rosenbrock.reference_full: Rosenbrock, H. H. (1960). An automatic method for finding the greatest or least value of a function. The Computer Journal, 3(3), 175-184.
Rosenbrock.notes: Banana function from optimization. Extremely high dynamic range; Sobol estimation requires N>10000. Chain of X_i,X_{i+1} interactions.

EnvironModel.name: EnvironModel
EnvironModel.primary_use_case: Contaminant transport; environmental risk assessment SA
EnvironModel.known_challenges: Log-normal input for M creates heavy-tailed output
EnvironModel.reference_full: Saltelli, A., Chan, K., & Scott, M. (Eds.). (2000). Sensitivity Analysis. Wiley.
EnvironModel.notes: 1D Gaussian plume model for contaminant diffusion. Physical model from environmental engineering. Log-normal inputs produce right-skewed output.

CSTRReactor.name: CSTRReactor
CSTRReactor.sensitivity_structure: Arrhenius exponential; rational steady-state; strong nonlinearity
CSTRReactor.primary_use_case: Chemical engineering process SA; Arrhenius kinetics sensitivity
CSTRReactor.known_challenges: Exponential in E_a/T creates very high nonlinearity; iterative solution for T
CSTRReactor.reference_title: Modeling with Differential Equations in Chemical Engineering
CSTRReactor.reference_full: Walas, S. M. (1991). Modeling with Differential Equations in Chemical Engineering. Butterworth-Heinemann.
CSTRReactor.notes: CSTR (Continuous Stirred Tank Reactor) steady-state model with Arrhenius kinetics. Physical energy balance solved iteratively. E_a dominates.

DetPep8D.name: DetPep8D
DetPep8D.sensitivity_structure: polynomial + logarithmic; decaying importance by dimension
DetPep8D.known_challenges: Monotonically decaying sensitivity structure by index
DetPep8D.reference_title: Generalized Latin hypercube design for computer experiments
DetPep8D.reference_full: Dette, H., & Pepelyshev, A. (2010). Generalized Latin hypercube design for computer experiments. Technometrics, 52(4), 421-429.
DetPep8D.notes: Dette-Pepelyshev 8D function designed for computer experiment design comparison. Naturally decaying sensitivity from X1 to X8.

Campbell2D.name: Campbell2D
Campbell2D.formula_latex: f(s_1, s_2; \xi) = \sin(c_1 \pi s_1) \sin(c_2 \pi s_2) + \theta_1 s_1 s_2
Campbell2D.known_challenges: Output depends on grid resolution; Sobol indices vary with spatial aggregation
Campbell2D.reference_title: Sensitivity analysis when model outputs are functions
Campbell2D.reference_full: Campbell, K., McKay, M. D., & Williams, B. J. (2006). Sensitivity analysis when model outputs are functions. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 91(10-11), 1468-1472.
Campbell2D.notes: 2D spatial benchmark for functional SA. Output is a surface f(s1,s2). COS (change-of-support) aggregation changes Sobol structure.

ExponentialCampbell2D.name: ExponentialCampbell2D
ExponentialCampbell2D.primary_use_case: Spatial SA with exponential structure; log-transform testing
ExponentialCampbell2D.known_challenges: Right-skewed output; COS aggregation highly sensitive to transform
ExponentialCampbell2D.reference_title: Sensitivity analysis when model outputs are functions
ExponentialCampbell2D.reference_full: Campbell, K., McKay, M. D., & Williams, B. J. (2006). Sensitivity analysis when model outputs are functions. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 91(10-11), 1468-1472.
ExponentialCampbell2D.notes: Exponential variant of Campbell 2D. Output is always positive; log transform restores additivity. Tests noncommutativity under log.

Campbell3D.name: Campbell3D
Campbell3D.formula_latex: f(s_1, s_2, s_3; \xi) = \sin(c_1 \pi s_1)\sin(c_2 \pi s_2)\sin(c_3 \pi s_3) + \theta s_1 s_2 s_3
Campbell3D.known_challenges: 3D grid; Sobol indices depend on resolution and aggregation operator
Campbell3D.reference_title: Sensitivity analysis when model outputs are functions
Campbell3D.reference_full: Campbell, K., McKay, M. D., & Williams, B. J. (2006). Sensitivity analysis when model outputs are functions. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 91(10-11), 1468-1472.
Campbell3D.notes: 3D generalisation of Campbell benchmark. Full 3-way interaction via triple product. Used for volumetric aggregation SA studies.

BoussinesqRecession.name: BoussinesqRecession
BoussinesqRecession.sensitivity_structure: power-law recession; strong nonlinearity in k
BoussinesqRecession.primary_use_case: Groundwater recession SA; functional sensitivity analysis
BoussinesqRecession.known_challenges: Time-varying sensitivity; early vs late recession dominated by different inputs
BoussinesqRecession.reference_title: Recherches theoriques sur l'ecoulement des nappes d'eau
BoussinesqRecession.reference_journal: Journal de Mathematiques Pures et Appliquees
BoussinesqRecession.reference_full: Boussinesq, J. (1904). Recherches theoriques sur l'ecoulement des nappes d'eau. Journal de Mathematiques Pures et Appliquees, 10, 5-78.
BoussinesqRecession.notes: Generalised power-law recession curve. Sensitivity shifts from Q0 (early) to k,Tr (late recession). Functional SA captures time-varying structure.

DampedOscillator.name: DampedOscillator
DampedOscillator.analytical_S1_formula: Analytical closed-form exists but complex; dominant m,k near resonance
DampedOscillator.primary_use_case: Structural/mechanical SA; functional sensitivity for dynamic systems
DampedOscillator.known_challenges: Sensitivity highly dependent on proximity to resonance (omega_f ~ sqrt(k/m))
DampedOscillator.reference_full: Saltelli, A., Ratto, M., Tarantola, S., & Campolongo, F. (2005). Sensitivity analysis for chemical models. Chemical Reviews, 105(7), 2811-2827.
DampedOscillator.notes: Linear 2nd-order ODE with harmonic forcing. Resonance at omega_f=sqrt(k/m) creates extreme sensitivity. Output is time-series displacement.

LotkaVolterra.name: LotkaVolterra
LotkaVolterra.sensitivity_structure: coupled ODE; oscillatory; all parameters interact
LotkaVolterra.primary_use_case: Ecology/population SA; coupled ODE system sensitivity
LotkaVolterra.known_challenges: Oscillatory output; period and amplitude both sensitive to parameters
LotkaVolterra.reference_full: Lotka, A. J. (1925). Elements of Physical Biology. Williams and Wilkins. / Volterra, V. (1926). Variazioni e fluttuazioni del numero d'individui in specie animali conviventi.
LotkaVolterra.notes: Classic predator-prey ODE. Oscillatory dynamics; period ~2pi/sqrt(alpha*gamma). SA reveals alpha,gamma dominate period; beta,delta dominate amplitude.

EpidemicSIR.name: EpidemicSIR
EpidemicSIR.sensitivity_structure: nonlinear ODE; R0=beta/gamma determines epidemic; threshold behavior
EpidemicSIR.primary_use_case: Epidemic modeling SA; infectious disease parameter sensitivity
EpidemicSIR.known_challenges: Threshold behavior at R0=beta/gamma=1; sharp sensitivity transition
EpidemicSIR.reference_title: A contribution to the mathematical theory of epidemics
EpidemicSIR.reference_journal: Proceedings of the Royal Society of London A
EpidemicSIR.reference_full: Kermack, W. O., & McKendrick, A. G. (1927). A contribution to the mathematical theory of epidemics. Proceedings of the Royal Society of London A, 115(772), 700-721.
EpidemicSIR.notes: Classic SIR compartmental model. Peak infected fraction I_max is highly sensitive to R0=beta/gamma. Widely used for COVID-19 policy SA.

HeatDiffusion1D.name: HeatDiffusion1D
HeatDiffusion1D.formula_latex: T(x,t) = T_L + (T_R-T_L)x + \sum_{n=1}^{N} \frac{2}{n\pi}[T_{init}-T_L-(T_R-T_L)x] \sin(n\pi x) e^{-\alpha n^2 \pi^2 t}
HeatDiffusion1D.sensitivity_structure: Fourier series; analytical solution; sensitivity shifts in time
HeatDiffusion1D.primary_use_case: PDE-based SA; functional sensitivity for heat transfer
HeatDiffusion1D.known_challenges: Output is (n_x, n_t) surface; functional SA required for full characterization
HeatDiffusion1D.reference_full: Fourier, J. B. J. (1822). Theorie analytique de la chaleur. Firmin Didot.
HeatDiffusion1D.notes: 1D heat equation with Dirichlet BCs solved analytically via Fourier series. Output is (n_x, n_t) temperature surface. Sensitivity dominated by BCs at early times, initial condition later.

Lorenz96.name: Lorenz96
Lorenz96.sensitivity_structure: chaotic; butterfly-effect; extreme sensitivity to initial conditions
Lorenz96.primary_use_case: Data assimilation; ensemble SA; chaotic system sensitivity
Lorenz96.known_challenges: Sensitivity diverges exponentially (Lyapunov); only meaningful for short time horizons
Lorenz96.reference_full: Lorenz, E. N. (1996). Predictability: A problem partly solved. In Proceedings of the Seminar on Predictability, ECMWF, Vol. 1, pp. 1-18.
Lorenz96.notes: Toy atmospheric model. F=8 is standard chaotic forcing. Positive Lyapunov exponent ~1.67. SA valid only for t < Lyapunov time (~0.6). Used in ensemble NWP SA.

TwoCompartmentPK.name: TwoCompartmentPK
TwoCompartmentPK.formula_latex: C(t) = A e^{-\alpha t} + B e^{-\beta t}
TwoCompartmentPK.analytical_S1_formula: Analytical AUC Sobol indices derivable from bi-exponential form
TwoCompartmentPK.primary_use_case: Drug dosing SA; PK model parameter sensitivity
TwoCompartmentPK.known_challenges: Discriminant of eigenvalue equation can be negative; clamped to 0
TwoCompartmentPK.reference_full: Gibaldi, M., & Perrier, D. (1982). Pharmacokinetics (2nd ed.). Marcel Dekker.
TwoCompartmentPK.notes: Standard two-compartment PK model. Dose D and volume V1 dominate Cmax. Rate constants k10,k12,k21 dominate decay phase. AUC Sobol analytically tractable.
